market_briefing.summary: Global markets show cautious stabilization on March 20, 2026, as oil prices ease from weekly highs following diplomatic de-escalation signals in the Middle East. Despite volatility, investors are monitoring hawkish central bank outlooks and persistent energy-driven inflation risks.
highlights: Brent crude oil eased to $107-$108/bbl after hitting $119, as diplomatic efforts attempt to stabilize the Strait of Hormuz.
US S&P 500 showed a modest recovery (+0.90%) on Friday, balancing out earlier weekly losses.
Indian markets rebounded strongly with Nifty50 reclaiming 23,300, tracking stabilized global energy cues.
Geopolitical instability remains the primary market driver, with focus on energy supply disruptions and central bank inflation responses.
Natural gas markets remain high-alert due to infrastructure damages in Qatar, keeping energy volatility elevated.
